description: Array manipulation problems covering common patterns

problems.name: Two Sum
problems.description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target. You may assume each input has exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
hints: Think about what you need to look up quickly
For each number, what are you looking for?
Can you use a hash map to track seen numbers?

solution.explanation: Use a hash map to store each number and its index. For each number, check if its complement (target - num) exists in the map.

follow_ups: What if the array is sorted?
What if there can be multiple pairs?
What if we need indices of all pairs?

problems.name: Maximum Subarray
problems.description: Given an integer array nums, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum.

examples.explanation: The subarray [4,-1,2,1] has the largest sum 6

hints: Consider what happens at each position
Should you continue the current subarray or start fresh?

solution.explanation: At each position, decide whether to extend the current subarray or start a new one. Track the maximum sum seen.

follow_ups: Can you solve it using divide and conquer?
What if you need to return the actual subarray?
What if the array contains only negative numbers?

problems.name: Merge Intervals
problems.description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
hints: What if you sort the intervals first?
How do you know if two intervals overlap?
Can you merge as you iterate?

solution.explanation: Sort by start time. For each interval, either extend the last merged interval or add a new one.

follow_ups: What if intervals are already sorted?
How would you insert a new interval?
What if you need to find intersection instead?

problems.name: Sliding Window Maximum
problems.description: Given an array nums and a sliding window of size k moving from left to right, return the max value in each window position.

examples.explanation: Window positions and max values shown

hints: Brute force is O(nk). Can you do better?
What if you maintained potential maximums in order?

solution.explanation: Maintain a deque of indices in decreasing order of values. The front is always the max of current window.

follow_ups: What if you need both min and max?
How would you handle a stream of data?
Can you solve this with a different data structure?
